################################################################################
# batch_ap.txt — gameplay C1->C2 drain (campaign batch 5 of 5 — FINAL)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-03.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool1,3,9,10,12,15 pre-assigned)
#               -> pool0,4,5,6 SKIPPED: leaked in-JVM locks during prior fanouts
#                  (see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock).
#               -> pool7,8 SKIPPED: POISONED during batch_an (in-JVM locks).
#               -> pool2 SKIPPED (NEW): threw a real LockException for ao_s2
#                  (2026-06-03) — treat as poisoned for the shared server's
#                  lifetime; do NOT re-assign without re-verification.
#               -> pool3 PROMOTED into the assignment: it was ao_s2's clean
#                  read-only fallback. pool11/13/14 carry stale-but-tolerable
#                  on-disk locks and are the first fallbacks.
# Total RVAs:   168  (ALL remaining gameplay-C1 rows: 0x00562460..0x0057ae30)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 28 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ak/al/am/an/ao.
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: gameplay C1 = 168 (was 792; al->636, am->480, an->324,
# ao->168). This batch takes ALL of them. After this slice gameplay C1 = 0 and
# the gameplay campaign CLOSES. Remaining C1 pools after ap: render (~458) +
# third-party-library residue (kept C1 by design).
#
# *** READ THIS — EXPECT AN EVEN HIGHER library_skip RATE THAN batch_ao. ***
# batch_ao (the slice immediately below this one, 0x47f450-0x562380) came back
# 27 plates / 129 library_skip (83%): the 0x00554-0x00562 pages are the vendored
# RenderWare-Physics-3.7 module. THIS batch's band (0x00562460-0x0057ae30) sits
# DIRECTLY BETWEEN that RW-Physics page and the qhull-2002.1 island
# (0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820, vendored VIA RenderWare Physics). Evidence already
# on file that this band is largely the same link unit:
#   * batch_aj reclassed 00562520 -> third-party-library[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]
#     ("bitfield-grid allocator", DAT_007d3ff8+0x114 calloc). s1's candidates
#     00562460/00562500/00562560/00562580/005625e0/00562600/00562660 are its
#     immediate neighbours.
#   * ao_s3's farfield-arena family cited FUN_00564190 as the RW-Physics
#     arena-destroy callee — that RVA is an s1 candidate here.
#   * The old batch-y C1 plates labeled this whole band "collision-bvh-octree +
#     physics-math tail" (re/analysis/bucket_00554010/ et al.) — BVH/octree
#     collision structures are the RW-Physics farfield/narrow-phase data side.
# CONSEQUENCE: it is plausible that MOST or ALL 168 are vendored RW-Physics
# (-> library_skip, kept C1) and the plated count is small. That is a SUCCESS,
# not a failure — every candidate leaves gameplay C1 either way and the
# campaign closes. Do NOT force-plate vendored code to inflate the count.
# BUT do not blanket-skip by address either: game-side collision GLUE that
# reads/writes game state (entity pools, 0x006xxxxx/0x007xxxxx game globals,
# application-page callers) may be interleaved — the per-RVA library-confirm
# rule decides, exactly as in batch_ao (which found 27 genuine rows inside an
# 83%-vendored slice, including the hud font-vector module).
#
# HOW TO DECIDE per RVA (library-confirm rule — apply it AGGRESSIVELY):
#   - If the function decodes as a named RenderWare/CRT/qhull primitive, OR Ghidra
#     shows a "Library Function: <sym>" tag, OR it is an obvious thin engine
#     primitive (solver/BVH/octree node ops, farfield/broad-phase pair mgmt,
#     matrix/vector math on parameter-passed records, RW allocator-table
#     DAT_007d3ff8 +0x108/+0x10c/+0x114/+0x118 usage with 0x309xx memhints,
#     qh_*-shaped convex-hull code) with NO game-state read/write:
#       -> do NOT plate it. Put it in the queue row's
#          `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>` field (tag = renderware |
#          RenderWare-Physics-3.7 | msvc-crt | qhull-2002.1). Do NOT rename it
#          on master. Central re-classify reclasses it to
#          third-party-library[<tag>] and keeps it C1.
#   - Only GENUINE game/engine-GLUE that reads/writes game state goes in `rvas=`
#     for the C1->C2 plate. When in doubt about library-vs-glue, PLATE it C1->C2
#     and record subsystem_observed + a bare [UNCERTAIN] — do NOT silently drop
#     it (ao_s3's 00558b40 is the precedent: library-shaped math, zero engine
#     callers -> plated gameplay with origin doubt recorded).
#   - qhull watch: the island officially starts at 0x0057c5b0, but s6's tail
#     (0x00578xxx-0x0057ae30) is adjacent. If a candidate decodes qh_*-shaped
#     (facet/ridge/vertex sets, qh global struct), tag library_skip=<rva>:qhull-2002.1.
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-03):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_ao fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-2210; drain 21c4dce1 + release + re-classify commits).
#     gameplay C1 324 -> 168. SAFE to fan out. (If a sweep is running when you
#     start, STOP and re-confirm.)
#   * Calibrated-band screen over all 168 candidates: ZERO hits in the three
#     CALIBRATED bands (CRT 0x004a0000-0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-
#     0x004fc9e0 | qhull 0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820 — s6 ends at 0x0057ae30, just
#     below the island). The vendored RW-Physics code in this band lives OUTSIDE
#     the calibrated bands and is caught by the per-RVA library-confirm rule.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj..ao
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — the
#     sweep manifest builder returns 0 plated for a bucket otherwise. HARD
#     requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=`). List vendored ones in a SEPARATE
#     `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...` field for the central re-classify (they are
#     NOT plated by the sweep). EXPECT this field to dominate this batch. If a
#     session plates ZERO rows, write `rvas=NONE` (the builder skips the row;
#     the library_skip list is still the deliverable — ao_s4/s5/s6 precedent).
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section; central re-classify mints from the
#     reserved range (this batch: U-8900..U-9199, S-7000..S-7199).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_ap s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (sibling parallel sessions
#     share the working copy; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FUZZY-GREP GOTCHA (memory; seen in am_s5 + an_s6):
#   A naive `grep <rva> hooks.csv` fuzzy-matches your RVA to unrelated C2 rows
#   that merely CITE it in the notes column. Confirm each candidate's OWN row
#   with an ANCHORED grep `^<rva>,` before deciding it is/isn't drift.
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (plated total will likely be well under 168 — expected, not an error).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; honor
#        subsystem_observed; reclass-OUT every library_skip RVA to
#        third-party-library[<tag>] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs from the reserved
#        ranges. gameplay C1 -> 0; campaign CLOSED. Next: plan the render
#        campaign (~458 C1, needs library-aware batches).
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ap_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). pool0/2/4/5/6/7/8 are SKIPPED (see header). If your
#   assigned slot throws a real LockException, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free
#   clone (try pool11/13/14 — stale-lock-tolerant — then any fresh clone), and
#   record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row. ALWAYS program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 168: gameplay (their current hooks.csv label). Per
#   the warning above, EXPECT the dominant share to be RenderWare-Physics-3.7
#   vendored code (-> library_skip, kept C1), a possible qhull fringe at the s6
#   tail, and a small share of genuine collision/world glue (plate C1->C2;
#   subsystem_observed may be gameplay/track-loader/render). CONFIRM per RVA from
#   decomp and record subsystem_observed=<X> / library_skip in the queue row.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_gameplay_00562460_00565120  (promote-c2, 28-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 28)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00562460_00565120/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First slice of the FINAL gameplay batch. Span 0x00562460-0x00565120: directly
above the batch_ao all-skip RW-Physics page. 00562460..00562660 are immediate
neighbours of confirmed-library 00562520 (bitfield-grid allocator); 00564190 was
cited by ao_s3 as the RW-Physics farfield-arena destroy callee. EXPECT heavy
library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]. Read each end-to-end; apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.
Confirm/reclassify each; do NOT silently drop.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (anchored `^<rva>,` grep — see fuzzy-grep gotcha). Deepen, don't restart.
  Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: ao_s3/s4 fragments (re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s3.md/s4.md) for the
  RW-Physics family idioms (DAT_007d3ff8 fn-table, 0x30900 memhint, 0xffff
  sentinels, scene/body offsets) and batch_ak's 00559c40 PhysicsSceneBodyRegister row.

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (header). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ap_s1";
   use Mashed_pool1 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Apply the library-confirm rule. For a genuine-glue RVA: write
   re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00562460_00565120/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-03) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint). For a vendored RVA: do NOT plate;
   add it to library_skip=<rva>:<tag> in the queue row.
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s1.md: date, ap_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   PLATED only, or NONE>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed +
   library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_ap s1): author <K> C1->C2 plates
   for gameplay 00562460_00565120".

# Candidates (28)
00562460,00562500,00562560,00562580,005625e0,00562600,00562660,00562ed0,00562fd0,00563810,00563840,00563940,00563b00,00563c80,00563de0,00563df0,00563e70,00563f60,00564040,00564100,00564130,00564190,005641b0,00564310,005645b0,005646c0,00564c80,00565120

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA decodes as a library family — note in plate-or-queue, do NOT rename; set
  subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva>:<tag>.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_gameplay_00565160_00566c10  (promote-c2, 28-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool3 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire; stale on-disk .lock is
             tolerable — pool3 opened read-only cleanly as ao_s2's fallback. Fall
             back only on a real LockException.)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 28)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00565160_00566c10/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s2.md

# Mission
Second slice (0x00565160-0x00566c10), continuation of the same band. EXPECT
heavy library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7] (collision-BVH/octree node machinery
per the old batch-y label). Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only
genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2. Confirm/reclassify each.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ap_s2", Mashed_pool3,
bucket dir bucket_gameplay_00565160_00566c10/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_ap s2): author <K> C1->C2 plates
for gameplay 00565160_00566c10".

# Candidates (28)
00565160,005651b0,00565200,00565260,00565550,00565570,00565590,005655a0,00565780,005659f0,00565ab0,00565bc0,00565c20,00565cd0,00565d50,00565ef0,00565fa0,00566050,00566200,005663a0,00566420,005665a0,005667c0,00566830,005668e0,00566aa0,00566b40,00566c10

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s3.md

# Mission
Third slice (0x00566ca0-0x00569e90). Same band; EXPECT heavy
library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]. Watch for genuine game-side collision
GLUE interleaved here (game globals / entity-pool access / application-page
callers) — plate those C1->C2 with subsystem_observed. Apply the library-confirm
rule per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ap_s3", Mashed_pool9, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00566ca0_00569e90/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s3.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ap s3): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00566ca0_00569e90".

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s5.md

Fifth slice (0x0056f020-0x00575b60). Same band, approaching the qhull island.
EXPECT heavy library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]; watch for the first
qh_*-shaped convex-hull functions (tag qhull-2002.1 if so). Apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

================================================================================
Session 6 — bucket_gameplay_00575c60_0057ae30  (promote-c2, 28-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool15 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 28)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00575c60_0057ae30/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth/last slice (0x00575c60-0x0057ae30) — the tail of the gameplay campaign,
ending 0x178 bytes below the qhull island boundary (0x0057c5b0). EXPECT heavy
library_skip; this close to the island, qh_*-shaped candidates are LIKELY — tag
those library_skip=<rva>:qhull-2002.1 (do NOT hand-plate; memory
project_qhull_rwphysics_island). Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA; plate
only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2. Closes the gameplay campaign 5/5.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ap_s6", Mashed_pool15, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00575c60_0057ae30/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ap_s6.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ap s6): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00575c60_0057ae30".

# Candidates (28)
00575c60,00575fe0,00576640,00576880,00577be0,00577cb0,00577ec0,005784a0,00578610,00578b20,00578bd0,00578cb0,00578d90,00578e50,00578ec0,00578f20,00578ff0,00579b50,00579c00,00579d50,00579e50,00579ee0,0057a250,0057a660,0057a9a0,0057adb0,0057ae20,0057ae30

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


################################################################################
# END batch_ap.txt — 168 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions (gameplay campaign
# 5/5 — FINAL). After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see
# header), then central re-classify C1->C2 + reclass-OUT every library_skip RVA
# to third-party-library[...] (kept-C1) + merge per-session queue fragments into
# re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained. gameplay C1 168 -> 0; the gameplay campaign
# CLOSES (15 subsystems at C1=0). The plated count will likely be far below 168
# (batch_ao precedent: 27/156) — that is expected and correct. Remaining C1
# after this: render (~458; needs library-aware batches — RW-core pages
# interleave there too) + third-party-library residue (kept C1 by design).
################################################################################
